message:
Code quality: clippy fixes, timer overflow safety, parking_lot migration

Sweep of code quality improvements across 16 files in runtime, network,
service, and test subsystems:

Bug fix:
- src/runtime/timer.rs: Replace arithmetic `+= 1` with wrapping_add(1)
  on generation counter to prevent panic at u64::MAX. Add test that
  exercises the wrap-around path with two entries at the same deadline.

Clippy underscore-prefix cleanup (variables used in tracing macros):
- src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs: _reward → reward
- src/runtime/scheduler/worker.rs: _worker_id → worker_id (2 sites)
- src/runtime/waker.rs: _source_label → source_label
- src/lab/runtime.rs: _old_time → old_time, _error → error
- src/messaging/jetstream.rs: _err → err

Clippy lint suppressions:
- src/runtime/builder.rs: Allow result_large_err on from_toml methods
- src/http/h1/http_client.rs: Allow large_enum_variant on ClientIo
- src/database/postgres.rs: Allow cast_possible_wrap, cast_sign_loss,
  needless_pass_by_ref_mut, match_same_arms

Idiomatic simplifications:
- src/tls/acceptor.rs, src/tls/connector.rs: Replace match-on-Option
  with map_or(false, ...) for ALPN negotiation checks
- src/tls/types.rs: Collect CertificatePinSet directly from iterator
  instead of intermediate Vec
- src/service/service.rs: Replace match-on-Result with map_or_else for
  timeout handling
- src/observability/otel.rs: Move struct definition before Arc creation
  to resolve forward-reference ordering issue in test

TCP listener registration fix:
- src/net/tcp/listener.rs: Remove unnecessary drop(registration) before
  re-acquisition of the same mutex, and reuse the existing lock guard
  when storing the new registration to avoid a redundant lock cycle

Gen server test migration:
- src/gen_server.rs: Replace std::sync::Mutex with parking_lot::Mutex
  in all test code, removing 24 .lock().unwrap() calls (parking_lot
  mutexes are not poisonable, so .lock() returns the guard directly).
  No behavioral change; purely reduces test boilerplate and eliminates
  poison-panic risk in test teardown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
